display.purpose: Landing-page hero and large empty-state titles. Rare in product surfaces; reserve for moments where the page is essentially a wordmark.

headline.purpose: Page-level titles inside the app shell ('Transactions', 'Investments'). One per route.

title.purpose: Card titles, dialog titles, table column headers, section labels. The most-used 'important text' size.

body.purpose: Default body. Transaction descriptions, form helpers, sidebar items, table rows. Cap long-form prose at 65-75ch.

label.purpose: Metadata, badge text, the xs button size, form micro-labels. Use sparingly.

motion.name: transition-all
motion.purpose: Default for buttons. Color, ring, and translate-y unify under one short transition.

motion.name: transition-colors
motion.value: color 150ms ease-out, background-color 150ms ease-out, border-color 150ms ease-out
motion.purpose: For inputs and any control where only the color changes on state.

motion.name: ease-out-quart
motion.purpose: Reserved easing for any motion longer than 150ms (sheet open, drawer slide). No bounce, no elastic.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Primary action button. Beaver Pelt Amber fill with warm-brown text. 28px tall, 12px font, square corners.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; height: 28px; padding: 0 8px; background: oklch(0.852 0.199 91.936); color: oklch(0.421 0.095 57.708); font-family: 'Inter Variable', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.625; letter-spacing: 0; border: 1px solid transparent; border-radius: 0; background-clip: padding-box; cursor: pointer; transition: all 150ms ease-out; outline: none; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: oklch(0.852 0.199 91.936 / 0.8); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { border-color: oklch(0.708 0.008 92); box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px oklch(0.708 0.008 92 / 0.3); } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(1px); } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.5; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: Secondary action. Hairline border on Paper White. The neutral choice for non-destructive secondary CTAs.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; height: 28px; padding: 0 8px; background: oklch(1 0.002 92); color: oklch(0.145 0.005 92); font-family: 'Inter Variable', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.625; border: 1px solid oklch(0.922 0.005 92); border-radius: 0; cursor: pointer; transition: all 150ms ease-out; outline: none; } .ds-btn-outline:hover { background: oklch(0.922 0.005 92 / 0.5); } .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { border-color: oklch(0.708 0.008 92); box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px oklch(0.708 0.008 92 / 0.3); } .ds-btn-outline:active { transform: translateY(1px); }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Toolbar / dense-row button. Transparent at rest, fills with a muted background on hover. Used in sidebar items, table-row actions, command bars.
components.html: <button class="ds-btn-ghost" type="button"><svg class="ds-btn-ghost-icon" width="14" height="14" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M5 12h14"/><path d="M12 5v14"/></svg>New</button>
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 4px; height: 28px; padding: 0 8px; background: transparent; color: oklch(0.145 0.005 92); font-family: 'Inter Variable', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.625; border: 1px solid transparent; border-radius: 0; cursor: pointer; transition: all 150ms ease-out; outline: none; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { background: oklch(0.97 0.005 92); } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { border-color: oklch(0.708 0.008 92); box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px oklch(0.708 0.008 92 / 0.3); } .ds-btn-ghost:active { transform: translateY(1px); } .ds-btn-ghost-icon { flex-shrink: 0; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Standard text field. 28px tall, Pencil Grey border, 20% Pencil Grey wash fill. Square corners. Focus shifts the border to Graphite Mid and adds a subtle ring.
components.css: .ds-input { display: block; width: 100%; min-width: 0; height: 28px; padding: 0 8px; background: oklch(0.922 0.005 92 / 0.2); color: oklch(0.145 0.005 92); font-family: 'Inter Variable', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 1.4; border: 1px solid oklch(0.922 0.005 92); border-radius: 0; outline: none; transition: color 150ms ease-out, background-color 150ms ease-out, border-color 150ms ease-out, box-shadow 150ms ease-out; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: oklch(0.556 0.008 92); } .ds-input:focus-visible { border-color: oklch(0.708 0.008 92); box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px oklch(0.708 0.008 92 / 0.3); } .ds-input:disabled { opacity: 0.5; cursor: not-allowed; } .ds-input[aria-invalid="true"] { border-color: oklch(0.577 0.245 27.325); box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px oklch(0.577 0.245 27.325 / 0.2); }

components.name: Card
components.description: Default container. Square corners, ring-1 hairline at 10% foreground. No shadow ever.
components.html:
November total
All accounts, USD
$4,283.17
components.css: .ds-card { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 16px; padding: 16px 0; background: oklch(1 0.002 92); color: oklch(0.145 0.005 92); border-radius: 0; box-shadow: inset 0 0 0 1px oklch(0.145 0.005 92 / 0.1); overflow: hidden; font-family: 'Inter Variable', system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-card-header { display: grid; grid-auto-rows: min-content; gap: 4px; padding: 0 16px; } .ds-card-title { font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.4; } .ds-card-description { color: oklch(0.556 0.008 92); font-size: 12px; line-height: 1.625; } .ds-card-content { padding: 0 16px; font-size: 12px; line-height: 1.625; } .ds-card-amount { font-feature-settings: 'tnum'; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; font-size: 24px; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: -0.01em; }

components.name: Sidebar Nav Item
components.description: Single sidebar entry. Active state fills with Burnished Gold, the only place that color appears in the chrome.
components.html: TransactionsSubscriptionsInvestments
components.css: .ds-sidebar { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 1px; padding: 8px; background: oklch(0.985 0.005 92); font-family: 'Inter Variable', system-ui, sans-serif; min-width: 200px; } .ds-sidebar-item { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; padding: 6px 8px; background: transparent; color: oklch(0.145 0.005 92); font-size: 12px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 1.625; text-decoration: none; border-radius: 0; transition: background-color 150ms ease-out, color 150ms ease-out; } .ds-sidebar-item:hover { background: oklch(0.97 0.005 92); } .ds-sidebar-item-active { background: oklch(0.681 0.162 75.834); color: oklch(0.987 0.026 102.212); font-weight: 500; } .ds-sidebar-item-active:hover { background: oklch(0.681 0.162 75.834); } .ds-sidebar-icon { flex-shrink: 0; }

components.name: Privacy-Mode Numeric
components.description: First-class state for any rendered financial value. The mask preserves layout (tabular-nums) so rows do not jump. Color is unchanged from the underlying value.
components.html:
Liquidity$2,418.03
Liquidity (privacy)••••.••
components.css: .ds-table { width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse; font-family: 'Inter Variable', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; line-height: 1.625; color: oklch(0.145 0.005 92); } .ds-row { border-bottom: 1px solid oklch(0.922 0.005 92); } .ds-row:last-child { border-bottom: none; } .ds-cell-label { padding: 8px 12px; color: oklch(0.556 0.008 92); } .ds-cell-amount { padding: 8px 12px; text-align: right; font-feature-settings: 'tnum'; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; font-weight: 500; } .ds-cell-private { color: oklch(0.145 0.005 92); letter-spacing: 0.05em; }

components.name: Account Color Legend
components.description: Reference for the seven semantic account colors. Every chart legend in the product uses exactly these swatch-and-label pairs.
components.html:
• Net Worth
  • Liquidity
  • Investment
  • Property
  • Receivable
  • Liability
  • Asset
components.css: .ds-legend { list-style: none; padding: 0; margin: 0; display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 8px 16px; font-family: 'Inter Variable', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 10px; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.4; letter-spacing: 0.02em; color: oklch(0.556 0.008 92); } .ds-legend-item { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; } .ds-swatch { display: inline-block; width: 8px; height: 8px; border-radius: 0; flex-shrink: 0; }

narrative.overview: Beaver Money's interface is a craftsman's bench: tools within reach, columns straight, surfaces uncluttered, a single warm amber that reads as worked wood and brass instrument. Every component earns its space. Numbers are the substance; chrome is the box. The user is logging by hand because logging by hand is the point. The system supports that ritual rather than decorating around it. Density is the policy, not an accident. Default rows are tight, targets are real but compact, and there is no breathing room offered 'to look modern.' The aesthetic lineage runs through Linear, Raycast, Stripe Dashboard, Beancount, and the better personal-finance managers without inheriting their consumer-fintech polish. We sit closer to a measurement instrument than to a marketing page. This system explicitly rejects every visual idiom that signals 'fintech' at a glance: gradient hero metrics, navy-and-gold legacy bank chrome, neon-on-black crypto trading, glassmorphism, soft-pastel SaaS-cream, gamified streaks, and identical icon-heading-text card grids.
keyCharacteristics: Sharp corners. --radius: 0 is the load-bearing decision; the entire scale collapses to zero.
Single warm hue (92°) tints every neutral. There is no cool grey anywhere in this product.
Rings, not shadows. Depth is conveyed by ring-1 ring-foreground/10 and tonal layering.
One typeface (Inter Variable). Hierarchy by scale and weight, never by family.
Semantic chart palette is fixed and meaningful. Colors map to account categories, not decoration.
Privacy-mode masking is a first-class state. Every numeric value must support it.

rules.name: The Warm-Neutrals Rule
rules.body: Every neutral, in both themes, carries chroma 0.005-0.01 at hue 92°. There is no #000, no #fff, no cool grey, no slate. If a designer reaches for a neutral, it must come from this palette or be derived by adjusting only lightness on hue 92°.

rules.name: The Semantic-Chart Rule
rules.body: The seven account colors are bound to their category for the lifetime of the product. They are not a generic 'data viz palette' to be reassigned per chart. A new chart picks colors from this list by what it represents, never by aesthetic preference.

rules.name: The One-Amber-At-A-Time Rule
rules.body: The main brand amber and the sidebar burnished gold do not appear on the same surface in the same role. Sidebar gold marks chrome (active nav); brand amber marks content actions. Mixing them weakens both.

rules.name: The One-Family Rule
rules.body: A second typeface is not added to introduce hierarchy. If two pieces of text need to be visually distinct, change weight, scale, color, or column position, never the family.

rules.name: The Tabular-Numerics Rule
rules.body: Any column of numbers (account balances, transaction amounts, investment values, currency conversions) renders with font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums. Decimals align by point, the eye scans down a column, totals are reconcilable at a glance. A misaligned decimal in a financial table is a bug, not a style preference.

rules.name: The Compact-Default Rule
rules.body: Default body is 12px (text-xs/relaxed). Designers do not bump it to 14px because the page 'feels too dense'; the density is the design. If a screen is genuinely overpacked, reduce content, not increase type.

rules.name: The No-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Drop shadows are forbidden across the product. If a designer believes a surface needs to 'lift,' the answer is a ring border or a tonal-layer adjustment, not a shadow.

rules.name: The Ring-Hairline Rule
rules.body: When a card needs an edge, it gets ring-1 ring-foreground/10. The ring sits inside the box; it does not affect layout; it does not change opacity on hover. It is the standard load-bearing edge for any container that holds tabular data.

rules.name: The Compact-Target Rule
rules.body: Buttons and inputs are 28px tall by default. Touch-priority surfaces may opt into 32-44px, but desktop never exceeds 28px without explicit reason. The hit area extends invisibly via padding for accessibility, but the visible chrome stays compact.

rules.name: The State-By-Color Rule
rules.body: State changes (hover, focus, active, disabled, invalid) shift color, ring, and at most a 1px translate. They do not animate scale, blur, opacity-on-translate, or any layout property.

dos: Do keep --radius: 0. The squared aesthetic is the load-bearing precision signal.
Do tint every neutral toward hue 92° with chroma 0.005-0.01.
Do use OKLCH for color values in styles.css. Match the existing token format; never split the source of truth into hex/HSL/RGB.
Do apply font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums to any column of numbers.
Do use the seven-color semantic account palette consistently. Net worth is always green; liabilities are always red; investments are always amber-orange.
Do use ring-1 ring-foreground/10 to give cards an edge.
Do keep buttons and inputs at h-7 (28px) by default.
Do use Lucide for every icon. Single-family icon set.
Do make privacy-mode masking work for any new financial value rendered.
Do respect prefers-reduced-motion, especially for any transition over 150ms.
donts: Don't use #000 or #fff anywhere. Every neutral must carry the warm hue tint.
Don't introduce box-shadow for elevation. The product is flat with rings; no exceptions.
Don't use background-clip: text with a gradient. No gradient text, no gradient hero metrics, no 'background gradient meets soft glow.' Solid colors only.
Don't introduce a second typeface. Inter Variable does all the work. Hierarchy is scale + weight.
Don't add cool greys, slate, navy, or 'cyberpunk' neon. Even the dark theme stays warm-neutral.
Don't use a border-left or border-right greater than 1px as a colored stripe accent on cards, alerts, or list items. This is the absolute-banned side-stripe pattern.
Don't repurpose the seven account colors for non-categorical decoration. They are bound to their account category for the life of the product.
Don't wrap every metric in a card. Tabular and inline displays are usually correct; the card is the lazy answer.
Don't show identical icon-heading-text card grids. The landing page already brushes against this; no further surfaces should adopt the pattern.
Don't imply auto-import is 'coming soon' or that manual entry is a fallback. Manual is the feature; the design must say so.
Don't gamify. No streaks, no confetti, no green/red 'you saved!' dopamine. The reward for logging is the awareness, not the animation.
Don't use glassmorphism. Backdrop-filter is reserved for the rare case of overlay readability; it is never decorative.
Don't bump default body size up 'to feel more breathable.' Density is the design.
